page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier for general compute and memory tasks. The Intel Xeon processor delivers average single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, with notable support for AVX-512 vector instructions. Memory read bandwidth reaches 413.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 52.02 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and complex tasks like ray tracing and image processing fall into the weak performance tier. Large language model inference is also a bottleneck, with prompt processing on a 7B model scoring in the poor tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highmem-8 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive CPU scaling. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis (which achieves 7.13 million operations per second in benchmarks), static web serving, and software compilation. It is less suitable for heavy parallel decompression, graphics rendering, or large-scale AI inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis operations reaching 7.13 million per second. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression, image processing, and LLM inference. Qualitatively, it provides a cost-effective balance for memory-intensive applications that do not demand extreme CPU scaling. It is recommended for database hosting, caching layers, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It delivers solid average performance for databases, caching, and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and average database performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio for database operations, caching, and general-purpose memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core.
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching.
Supports AVX-512 vector instructions for specialized workloads.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications.
